frameworks.name: deep reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: neural networks trained via deep RL posed as models of human cognitive flexibility

frameworks.name: habit-goal arbitration
frameworks.one_line: networks learn complementary habit and goal-based policies

frameworks.name: meta-learning
frameworks.one_line: flexibility meta-learned from large diverse pre-training enabling in-context adaptation

core_question: How can deep neural networks be made to exhibit human-like cognitive flexibility rather than converging to a fixed solution?
key_assumptions: standard deep RL converges to a fixed policy that limits adaptation
human cognitive flexibility can be captured by network-level training regimes
flexibility arises from training procedure, not just architecture
manipulations: training regime: dual habit vs goal-based policies
pre-training data diversity for meta-learned in-context adaptation
level of agent control over the environment during meta-training
key_findings: networks can be trained to acquire complementary habit and goal-based policies
meta-learning from large diverse data enables in-context adaptation to novel inputs
deep networks can be meta-trained to adjust behaviour to their level of environmental control
interpretations: three training paradigms cast deep networks as viable models of human cognitive flexibility
flexibility in neural networks can be induced by training, overcoming the fixed-solution limitation
